title: Artisanal Emoji
speaker: Myke
text: If anybody is ever gonna make a Connected clip show, 'Everyone Needs a Hobby' is potentially my favorite clip of all time. That was the sickest of burns.
note: Stephen predicts a clip show and names his favorite moment — almost too perfect as an opener

title: S-Tier Jason Snell
speaker: Federico
text: We're gonna do new content and not because we're gonna do like a clip show of the best moments of six hundred episodes of Connected. Who likes a clip show? No one. Okay, thank you.
note: Federico dismisses clip shows entirely — one episode before the clip show launched — giving this the same perfect energy as the ep 37 opener

title: Stabbed by a Glass Corner
speaker: Federico
text: Ah, we did it. You can't do that to us, Myke. You have to do it. One at a time.
note: Federico furious that Myke introduced both guests at once instead of one at a time

title: Stabbed by a Glass Corner
speaker: Myke
text: They're also dropping the Nokia brand and they're gonna buy Minecraft. For two billion dollars. So maybe they're just gonna make the Windows interface look like Minecraft.
note: Myke and Federico riffing on Microsoft's chaotic acquisition strategy

title: Nothing Was Sacred
speaker: Federico
text: Our friend Myke is in Italy and he decided to not visit me. He's in my same country, but for some reason he doesn't want to see me. He's in a completely different part of Italy. Several hours apart. And I totally understand because the part he's in right now is much better than where I live.
note: Federico guilt-tripping Myke for being in Italy without visiting, then undermining himself completely

title: The Divine Comedy of Homescreens
speaker: Federico
text: I mean, if Dante were to write the Divine Comedy these days, I'm pretty sure the rearranging icons on the iOS home screen would be a circle of hell.
note: Federico elevates iOS home screen icon rearrangement to a Dante-level circle of hell

title: Squidges
speaker: Myke
text: It also does a one-handed keyboard where it kind of like squidges the keyboard into the bottom right. Squidges. I'm going with squidges. I can't think what else you'd say. Squishes? No. Squidges.
note: The birth of the word 'squidges' — which became the episode title

title: An Italian Stephen
speaker: Federico
text: So maybe, you know, I'm becoming in a way an Italian Stephen.
note: Federico realising he's accumulating old hardware just like Stephen — the episode title moment

title: The Old Mac Paladin
speaker: Myke
text: Force Touch and Digital Crown are the two worst names Apple has ever given anything.
note: Myke's verdict on Apple's Apple Watch naming, delivered with complete certainty

title: On Principle, I Shunned These Ideas
speaker: Federico
text: On principle, I would have shunned these ideas. Especially the blue line — I have no interest in you, leave me alone, never come back. It's just so dumb. What are you doing? Do you work for Twitter, by the way? I just want to double check. You don't work for them, right? Okay cool. I really don't.
note: Federico's rant against Twitter features he despises — but has apparently been using anyway

title: Conceptually Sad
speaker: Federico
text: About this — not physically sad, but just like conceptually sad. If that's a thing.
note: Federico inventing a new emotional register: conceptually sad. The episode title.

title: Click on the Blue E
speaker: Myke
text: Here's what you gotta do. Go down yonder to the fancy City Boy coffee shop. Ask the nice young man to show you the computer box in the back room. They call it Internet Cafe. Click on the blue E on the background. Now it's gonna make some noise like robots making love, but eventually you'll be able to type.
note: Myke's fake-Southern tutorial on how to use Internet Explorer — the episode title

title: 50% My Duty
speaker: Federico
text: It's nineteen forty-five in Tennessee. Uh, in some ways that's not untrue.
note: Myke roasting Stephen about Tennessee being stuck in the past; Stephen's response makes it

title: 50% My Duty
speaker: Myke
text: So what did you do at like, at midnight? Did you just stare at the OS X installer? Oh, I was in bed.
note: Myke asking what Stephen did for New Year's Eve, assuming it involved a Mac

title: 50% My Duty
speaker: Federico
text: Yeah, Phil Schiller's just outside someone's house with a baseball bat waiting for them to come out.
note: Federico's Godfather-style explanation of Apple's app rejection policy

title: Crazy Uncle Figure
speaker: Stephen
text: I'm gonna write that down — that you apparently can buy old computers. And I'm gonna look into this for next week.
note: Stephen sarcastically 'writing down' Myke's revelation that vintage Macs can be purchased

title: Crazy Uncle Figure
speaker: Stephen
text: I do have an exterior garage workshop that I could turn into a museum. Or I could put the family out there and turn the house into a museum. Both really good ideas.
note: Stephen seriously considering evicting his family to house his vintage Mac collection

title: Artisanal Emoji
speaker: Federico
text: I don't want these little shaking guys. I just want my traditional, classical, artisanal emoji.
note: Federico coining 'artisanal emoji' — the phrase that named the episode

title: Artisanal Emoji
speaker: Federico
text: But maybe other people feel like they wanna be analysts and they want to kinda tell Apple what to do. And that's also fine because everybody has a hobby.
note: Federico's devastating dismissal of iPad critics — the burn that Stephen immediately declares his favorite clip of all time

title: Seven Minutes of Empty Exercise
speaker: Federico
text: So I set the watch to an outdoor walk workout and I walked alone on my balcony for seven minutes until I filled the green circle. So if you happen to be one of my neighbors from the other buildings and you were watching a person walking on the balcony very intensively for seven minutes — that was me. And I'm not crazy. I was just trying to fill my exercise rings.
note: Federico doing laps on his balcony to close his Watch rings — the episode title

title: Random Pressure Lottery
speaker: Federico
text: It's just that Stephen has the voice of an adult, whereas you're more like... you know, when you watch a TV show and you can tell it's not an American TV show? That's kind of what you are.
note: Federico's devastating comparison of Myke's voice to a British MTV show, delivered with love

title: Tim is the New Cook
speaker: Federico
text: It was the summer of the selfie stick. Everybody — literally every group of friends had one. There were people taking selfies into the sea with a waterproof case. Groups of ten people. Couples at restaurants. At one point I saw someone using the selfie stick as a walking stick in Positano. That felt quite original as a use case.
note: Federico's epic oral history of selfie sticks on Italian vacation

title: Tim is the New Cook
speaker: Federico
text: What's even more ridiculous is that I forgot to bring my selfie stick.
note: The perfect punchline: Federico observed the selfie stick phenomenon obsessively, then forgot his own

title: Whatever it's Called This Week
speaker: Federico
text: I'm like a robot and all I can do is type.
note: Federico's bleak self-assessment of his iPad-only workflow

title: My Feeling About This is That I'm Jealous
speaker: Myke
text: BlaBlaCar has just raised a hundred and sixty million dollars of funding and have a valuation at one point two billion dollars. Which puts them in a unique class of businesses, and one that I think quite frankly we can take all the credit for. The Connected episode where I described the BlaBlaCar service clearly pushed investors over the edge and convinced them to give BlaBlaCar money.
note: Myke claiming full personal credit for BlaBlaCar's $1.2B valuation based on one podcast mention

title: Wake up in a Gentle Mood
speaker: Federico
text: My girlfriend is a normal person and I'm lazy. I don't want to be woken up by an alarm, either from my phone or from my watch. I like to be woken up by my girlfriend with a smile and a cup of espresso. That's how I like to be working.
note: Federico's fully automated girlfriend-as-wake-up-service system, explained with complete sincerity

title: Driving on a Vespa in a Landscape of Pastel Colors
speaker: Myke
text: I could picture you driving on a Vespa in a landscape of pastel colors. It's a dream of mine, Federico. But you don't drive, so that's why it's all a dream.
note: The image that became the episode title, immediately undercut by the fact Federico can't drive

title: Siri Like the Butler
speaker: Federico
text: Kinda, basically, Siri like a butler, you know.
note: Federico's vision for HomeKit: Siri as a formal butler managing the household

title: The App Is Named App
speaker: Stephen
text: My app is named App.
note: Stephen's app for managing his vintage Mac collection. Its name is App. The episode title.

title: I'm Holding a Billboard
speaker: Federico
text: Safari in portrait is just like — holy cow — this thing. I'm like holding a billboard.
note: Federico's description of the iPad Pro's massive portrait screen — the episode title

title: Being in Charge of a Space Rocket
speaker: Federico
text: Being in charge of a space rocket.
note: Federico's description of managing his Workflow automation setup — gave the episode its title

title: Snoozepocalypse
speaker: Stephen
text: They're all gone forever and they're not coming back. We can all look forward to Snoozepocalypse on February 26th.
note: Stephen's apocalyptic farewell to the snooze button

title: What's a Heart, Really
speaker: Federico
text: I don't understand, Stephen, why you do anything that you do.
note: Federico's existential exasperation at Stephen's choices

title: Capital D for the Big Smile
speaker: Federico
text: I only care about emotions, you know, like smiles and hearts and monkeys for some reason.
note: Federico's complete emoji philosophy: smiles, hearts, and for reasons he cannot explain, monkeys

title: Explosion of Glue and Colors
speaker: Stephen
text: It's like an explosion of glue and colours.
note: Stephen's description of the iPhone 5C's color options — the episode title

title: I'm Like a Butterfly
speaker: Myke
text: I'm like a butterfly.
note: Zero context. No explanation. Just Myke, declaring himself a butterfly. Became the episode title.

title: All Things Pizza
speaker: Myke
text: I'm really proud that the business that you and I have built is the preeminent podcast network for all things pizza.
note: Myke fully leaning into the pizza tangent having taken over the episode

title: Pokédex of iMacs
speaker: Federico
text: No doubt there are people who are already writing emails to us saying this is ridiculous, just use a Mac — and I would tell you to stop writing that email.
note: Federico preemptively dismisses all criticism of his iPad-only workflow with maximum confidence

title: My Brain is Cruising eBay at Night
speaker: Stephen
text: My brain populated the shelves of this Dreamland Apple Museum with models and machines on my wish list. So basically my brain is cruising eBay at night, I guess, is what's happening now.
note: Stephen reveals his subconscious is just eBay, browsing vintage Macs while he sleeps

title: No Such Thing as a Sticker Emergency
speaker: Federico
text: There is no such thing as a sticker emergency.
note: Federico delivers the episode title as a calm, authoritative fact — immediately contradicted by everything around it

title: Bathtub Full of Espresso
speaker: Federico
text: I'm not in a bathtub full of espresso, but I feel like we're approaching that point quite quickly by now.
note: Federico's opening check-in, said with complete sincerity — the episode title

title: Riding Down a Hill on a Bicycle with Square Wheels
speaker: Myke
text: Um I it felt like that maybe they were riding down a hill with a bicycle with square wheels on it or something. Like I'm not a hundred percent sure what would cause that amount of uh wobbling. But if it was true, their video stabilization is insane in the membrane.
note: Myke invents the episode title on the spot trying to describe shaky Google Pixel footage — the analogy is absurd and self-aware

title: Here is Smart Phone
speaker: Federico
text: It basically just looks to me like smartphone. Like that's how you would call this. The design of this is smartphone. Like they asked, can we design smartphone? Yes, here is smartphone.
note: Federico's broken-English deconstruction of a generic Android phone is perfectly absurd — and became the episode title

title: Caramel
speaker: Federico
text: So CoreML, it sounds like Caramel, but it's uh it's it's not it's not that it's not Caramel 4. AI. It's a different thing.
note: Federico mishears Apple's CoreML as 'Caramel' and the confusion is so complete and earnest it became the episode title

title: The Turtle Anniversary
speaker: Federico
text: Yeah, but it could be called the the Turtle Anniversary instead of the nature anniversary. It implies that more turtles are keeping track of the other turtles that are together. So like it creates a whole ecosystem of turtles keeping track of this very specific anniversary.
note: Federico goes completely off the rails theorising about turtles maintaining anniversary records — the absurdity escalates line by line

title: Badman Ears
speaker: Myke
text: I love you so much, but it f to me it sounded like you said Batman, and I was like, ooh. Hang on a second. We got we got a bad man.
note: Federico says 'Batman ears' about the iPhone X notch with his Italian accent; Myke mishears it as 'badman' — the episode title

title: A Handbag Face Cream A Chainsaw
speaker: Stephen
text: There are some there are some people in my life, who are very southern and really struggle with Federico's name. And and sort of pronounce it if he's if he's if he's Fred? Fredrico? Frederico sounds like um, I don't know, maybe. Uh he sounds older, maybe like he's a barber or some sort of handyman.
note: Stephen's deadpan meditation on Southern people mangling Federico's name ends with the perfectly cruel 'barber or some sort of handyman'

title: Daily Dongle Carry
speaker: Federico
text: Hello Halbus Percival Horley.
note: Federico attempts Myke's elaborate full name and produces an entirely wrong string of nonsense syllables

title: Daily Dongle Carry
speaker: Stephen
text: Well not my daily dongle carry. No. My emergency dongles. Dongle reserve. Yeah, the dongle reserve is really good.
note: Stephen casually reveals he has both a 'daily dongle carry' AND a separate 'dongle reserve' — peak 2017 MacBook Pro adapter chaos

title: My Mother Has a Bunch of Keyboards
speaker: Federico
text: She really likes the original iPad dock with the keyboard. They're not making that anymore. Well you gave her a bunch of keyboards. She's still using Facebook for iOS 5.
note: Federico casually reveals he has gifted his mother multiple keyboards and she's running a years-old version of Facebook

title: Super Easy To Do a Top Case
speaker: Stephen
text: Put topcase on like the MacBook, you know, the white and black plastic MacBook. Super easy to do a top case.
note: Stephen's breezy enthusiasm about swapping a MacBook top case as if it's the most natural thing in the world — the episode title

title: My Bedroom Has Become a Museum
speaker: Stephen
text: My bedroom has sort of become like a museum. There's like a guided tour, and whenever a friend comes over, it's like — go visit my nightstand.
note: Stephen has turned his nightstand into a museum exhibit with guided tours — the episode title

title: Shazamalo
speaker: Federico
text: It's like it's it's Shazzammalo... Oh like uh like when when they when someone says tag it on Shazam but as a verb. And it's it's the the conjugation of the verb in Italian that is so awful. It's one of those Shazamolo.
note: Federico coins the absurd Italian verb conjugation for 'to Shazam a song' — the mangled result is so bad even he cracks up, and it became the episode title

title: A Kernel Panic at the Disco
speaker: Stephen
text: If your computer has a problem at a party, is it a kernel panic at the disco?
note: Stephen delivers a perfectly timed dad-joke tech pun that became the episode title — followed immediately by an off-mic groan of despair

title: The Tiny Head Trend
speaker: Federico
text: And uh we uh Stephen and Myke and I we call this trend the Tiny Heads trend... there's the same the same cartoon characters with large bodies and tiny heads... uh basically that type of uh structure but with a really tiny head.
note: Federico gravely introduces 'The Tiny Heads Trend' as if delivering a design industry report — treating the visually absurd as serious cultural analysis

title: Headspaced Against My Will
speaker: Myke
text: My fiance uses it too. Uh which means that I am forced to use it sometimes because she usually plays when she's going to sleep. So I'm forced into listening to it. I am head spaced against my will.
note: Myke's increasingly dramatic account of being involuntarily subjected to his fiancée's bedtime Headspace sessions — 'headspaced against my will' is the episode title

title: Wireless Charging Denier
speaker: Myke
text: I don't I'm not like a wireless charging denier, right? Because like there is someone in my household that uses it every single day, right? Like I don't think that it's bad. It's just it just doesn't do anything for me, like personally.
note: Myke protests he's not a 'wireless charging denier' while describing exactly what a denier would say — the episode title names him anyway

title: I Move My Arms Around
speaker: Federico
text: And uh for some reason I realize that I move my arms around when I do this. I don't stand perfectly still. Uh maybe I'm using the Apple Watch to control um podcast playback for example, or I just need to stretch my arms, but I move my arms around and the Apple Watch didn't like my wrist movements.
note: Federico solemnly investigates why his Apple Watch gives wrong heart rate readings — the culprit, delivered with full sincerity, is that he waves his arms

title: The Robot Should Keep Its Mouth Shut
speaker: Myke
text: Like the robot should keep its mouth shut in my opinion. Dumb robot. Who does he think he is? Also I don't want it to murder anybody.
note: Myke's passionate argument that home security robots should be silent and not commit murder

title: A Man of Movable Structure
speaker: Myke
text: You're you're a man of of movable structure, right? The structure is there, but you like to do it on your own terms.
note: Myke invents an instantly iconic piece of corporate nonsense to describe Stephen's flexible work style — 'a man of movable structure'

title: An Internal Fortnite
speaker: Federico
text: Yeah, seal should be following the battle royale rules. So sort of like a like an internal Fortnite is what I'm asking for.
note: Federico proposes Apple's software teams should compete battle-royale style to reduce features — peak Federico absurdist product management

title: NanoHippo
speaker: Federico
text: I would like to exclude myself from this narrative that I've not wanted to be a part of since two years.
note: Federico formally recusing himself from an ongoing bit with slightly broken English that makes it perfect

title: Numeric Professional
speaker: Federico
text: Um, which I I don't need a number pad because I'm not an accountant. Um I mean w even when I do my my taxes I don't need a number pad. I'm not that kind of a... Numeric professional.
note: Federico pauses hunting for the word 'accountant,' lands on 'numeric professional' instead — an accidental neologism that became the episode title

title: The Bear Will Charge You
speaker: Myke
text: I feel like you need a timid animal — like, it's otterish or something. I'm otterish on Apple Stock.
note: Myke invents 'otterish' as a new stock market position — the hosts don't understand bull vs bear but they're trying

title: The Year of Stephen
speaker: Stephen
text: The year of Stephen is here.
note: Stephen's grandly self-important proclamation — answered immediately by Federico declaring 'you are worse than Luigi'

title: The Unique Blend of Tattoos and Automation
speaker: Federico
text: The unique blend of tattoos and automation. That's my my life in twenty nineteen.
note: Federico summarizes his entire 2019 identity in one sentence that is simultaneously accurate and utterly unhinged

title: The Unique Blend of Tattoos and Automation
speaker: Myke
text: So that's my app. It's called 123 Event Task Calendar Manager. And we'll get to in a minute why I have no plans to make it or have someone make it for me.
note: Myke names his app — a continuation of the 123 brand — then immediately announces he has no intention of ever building it

title: This is Not Propaganda
speaker: Myke
text: This is not propaganda.
note: Myke's flat deadpan interjection mid-Federico-speech — the timing makes it sound precisely like something someone would say if it absolutely were propaganda

title: Federighi Snapped His Fingers
speaker: Stephen
text: The phones slowly evaporate out of their cases the night iOS 13 comes out. It's like the it underscore just snaps his finger fingers on all those phones. Well I think I think Craig Federigi snapped his finger.
note: Stephen riffs on iOS 13 dropping old iPhone support as a Thanos-snap by Craig Federighi — the nerd-culture crossover named the episode

title: Full Oracle Mode
speaker: Federico
text: This is like we have a we have an o we have like a an oracle and the oracle has spoken single word answers. It was like, hey Mr. Oracle, what do you think about this? Good.
note: Sick Myke is limited to one-word answers all episode; Federico declares him a mystical Oracle — and Myke's deadpan 'Good' is perfect

title: Jeff Would Absolutely Destroy Me
speaker: Myke
text: Jeff looks like he could throw down. Jeff would absolutely destroy me in a fight. He would. He really would.
note: Myke's confident and completely unprompted assessment of his own physical inferiority to Jeff

title: Hot Salt
speaker: Myke
text: It's grain of hot salt.
note: Stephen warns to take a rumor 'with a Xeon-sized grain of salt'; Myke and Stephen riff on how hot Xeons run, until Myke mangles the idiom into the accidental episode title

title: Addicted to the Thrill of Cyber Crime
speaker: Stephen
text: Are you or someone you love addicted to the thrill of cybercrime? Call us at Norton.
note: Myke reads a dry hacker-motivation blurb; Stephen pivots instantly into a mock late-night infomercial, delivering the episode title as his punchline

title: You Handed Me a Watermelon
speaker: Stephen
text: It's like you handed me a watermelon.
note: Stephen describes holding a large iPhone in an oversized case with an analogy so physical and specific it became the episode title

title: Best Trees in the Country
speaker: Federico
text: Frederic has the best trees in the country, hands down.
note: Federico pitches 'Frederic's Christmas Trees,' his entirely fictional Utah tree-farm side-business, including reading a glowing testimonial from a satisfied customer — completely deadpan

title: Symbiosis, Osmosis, Whatever
speaker: Federico
text: So by you know by symbiosis or osmosis, whatever, um they're all they're good, equally good.
note: Federico waves off which scientific process applies to AirPod tip acoustics with 'whatever,' perfectly encapsulating his relationship with technical precision

title: Doing the Dance Again
speaker: Federico
text: No need to do the share opening dance again.
note: Federico coins 'the share opening dance' for iOS's share sheet workflow mid-tutorial, then uses it again moments later, sealing it as the episode title

title: stevensmerch.com
speaker: Federico
text: Today it picked up out of nowhere a phone number and started calling it. I had to rush to the HomePod and touch the top to say stop because I panicked. And Sylvia started yelling as usual at the thing and she told me: you make a decision — either you disable Siri, or I will unplug them all and throw them in the trash.
note: Federico's HomePod spontaneously starts calling random numbers; Sylvia issues a nuclear ultimatum

title: Rub a Dub Dub, My Friend
speaker: Stephen
text: The heart wants what the heart wants, and the heart wanted a G4 Cube over the 13-inch MacBook Air.
note: Stephen justifying an irrational vintage Mac purchase using the language of romantic desire

title: You Strike Me as a Wheel Guy
speaker: Myke
text: Four wheels, so that's a hundred and seventy-five dollars a wheel. $700 for four wheels. They don't even have brakes. This just rolls away.
note: Myke on Apple's $700 Mac Pro wheels that cannot stop rolling — the value proposition

title: The Scrooge McDuck of Computers
speaker: Myke
text: There are over a hundred computers in Stephen's house. For essentially you and your wife — because you have three kids, and your wife is likely only gonna use like a MacBook and an iPad — so you have a hundred and one computers for yourself.
note: Myke methodically calculating that Stephen has ~101 computers allocated to himself personally

title: Software, Firmware, Situation, Update
speaker: Stephen
text: Bridge had a software, firmware, situation update uh that came out.
note: Stephen tries to describe a keyboard firmware patch and accidentally stacks 'software,' 'firmware,' and 'situation' into a single compound noun, inventing the episode title

title: You Bought a Monument
speaker: Myke
text: I'm a big mozzarella boy. That's what I am. I'm milky and white.
note: Myke's self-portrait using Italian cheese — a description of his winter pallor that Federico started

title: You Bought a Monument
speaker: Myke
text: You didn't buy a computer, you bought a monument.
note: Myke's one-line verdict on Stephen's Mac Pro with stand purchase — perfect and devastating

title: You Cannot Put a Price on Glory
speaker: Federico
text: The Rickies entail glory, and you cannot put a price on glory.
note: Myke's solemn defense of the Connected awards on their 300th episode

title: We Should be Doing the Questioning
speaker: Federico
text: I feel like we should be doing the questioning, not US politicians.
note: Federico sincerely argues that Connected should be running the Congressional hearing on Apple rather than actual US lawmakers — and Myke immediately, earnestly agrees

title: Big Shoes to Phil
speaker: Stephen
text: And so I think he's got big shoes to fill, but I think he's up for the job.
note: Stephen delivers the Phil Schiller / 'fill' pun with complete innocence — apparently not noticing he said 'big shoes to fill' about a man named Phil

title: A Honeycomb of Circuits and Coils
speaker: Federico
text: Like it's just a it's just a bunch of coils, right? It's not like uh a honeycomb of circuits and and coils like that looks super complex.
note: Federico describes the interior of a third-party wireless charger with unintentional grandeur, producing a phrase that sounds like a prog-rock album title

title: Drop of Water in a Desert
speaker: Myke
text: This is like a very like this is almost like like a drop of water to somebody in a desert, right? Where we are with this whole mess right now.
note: Myke reaches for a dramatic desert-survival metaphor to describe Apple's tiny concession on App Store transparency, fully committing to the desperation of the image

title: Trojan Horse in My Phone
speaker: Myke
text: Federico, you've put a Trojan horse in my phone again. Now my iCloud's gonna get messed up.
note: Myke treats Federico's latest Shortcuts recommendation as a full cyberattack on his phone — a perfectly melodramatic exasperated-Myke moment

title: I Overspeakered Myself
speaker: Federico
text: I over-speakered myself, I think. I realized I have three HomePods and I recently realized that I had a Sonos hidden behind my bedroom television that I never used and I have another Sonos in storage and I have um one two … four unused Amazon Echo devices.
note: Federico performs a live involuntary inventory of his forgotten speaker collection, cataloging an absurd accumulation of HomePods, Sonos speakers, and Echo devices nobody knew he still owned

title: Captain Flip Flop
speaker: Myke
text: You flip flopped already. Captain Flip Flop. I am putting out a call to our listeners — somebody needs to make a timeline of Stephen's flip-flopping here.
note: Myke officially titles Stephen 'Captain Flip Flop' and calls for documentary evidence

title: ismh@hey.com
speaker: Stephen
text: ISMH on Twitter. You can find me on Hey, isMH at hey.com for the next 14 days until my trial ends and I never log in again.
note: Stephen reveals his Hey email address to listeners with an immediate built-in admission that he will stop using it in two weeks

title: Honey, Did You Put Kickstands on my Yoga Mat
speaker: Stephen
text: Honey, did you put uh kickstands on my yoga mat?
note: Stephen imagines the chaos of Apple making fitness accessories — voiced as a domestic complaint to a confused spouse; gave the episode its name

speaker: Stephen
text: One, two, three, four, five. There's six Power Macintoshes I can see plus an old style Mac Pro. So a lot.
note: Stephen counts the vintage Macs visible in his office mid-sentence as if this is entirely unremarkable — and reaches six

title: Why Are the Italians Involved
speaker: Myke
text: Why are the Italians involved?
note: Myke's baffled interjection during a design-history tangent — perfect with no context and gave the episode its name

title: If Robots Happen, We'll Need To Readdress This
speaker: Stephen
text: Okay, so Jason Snell gets a lifetime ban on flipping the coin, but digital voice assistance — if robots happen, we'll need to readdress this.
note: Stephen future-proofing the Rickies rules against AI robot contestants — his hedge clause became the episode title

title: A Setup of Optimism
speaker: Federico
text: And I'm just gonna use my iPad with the magic keyboard, as always. That was a setup of optimism, wasn't it?
note: Federico realises mid-sentence that his elaborate desk setup is already gone — then names the episode himself

title: Now You Drink Water Using a Balloon
speaker: Federico
text: Imagine if I told you you no longer drink water using a glass. Now you drink water using a balloon. It's like, wait, what — why — like I grew up drinking water from a glass.
note: Federico's analogy for an Apple UI paradigm shift escalates to balloon-based hydration — the absurdity gave the episode its name

title: I Need my Hands, Especially in the Summer
speaker: Federico
text: My hands were hurting, my fingers were all weird and my wrists were hurting and I needed to write the review and I thought — you know, I need my hands, especially in the summer.
note: Federico explaining why he stopped playing Nintendo DS during iOS review season — 'especially in the summer' is inexplicable and perfect

title: The Apple Maps of Cellular Radios
speaker: Stephen
text: The Apple maps of cellular radios is what you're saying.
note: Stephen coins a one-sentence metaphor for wildly inaccurate signal bars — it named the episode

title: Sorry, I'm Plastic
speaker: Federico
text: Sorry, sorry I'm plastic.
note: Federico's deadpan response to Apple's 'unapologetically plastic' iPhone 5C marketing — four words, episode title

title: MagTricky.com
speaker: Federico
text: Because of magtricky.
note: Federico reveals the existence of magtricky.com — a domain someone registered as a MagSafe joke that kept recurring in the episode

title: MacBook Venti
speaker: Myke
text: They should follow the Starbucks approach — they should make like a MacBook Venti, for example.
note: Myke applies Starbucks sizing to the MacBook lineup — the episode title

title: Federico Viticci and the Multiverse of Madness
speaker: Myke
text: I could usurp you. I could take my moment to usurp you, just to then introduce Federico. Instead of me introducing each of you, it's like an introduction chain.
note: Myke plots a coup against the traditional intro format, live, in real time

title: I'm a Creative Fountain
speaker: Federico
text: I'm a creative fountain today.
note: Federico's self-assessment, delivered with absolute sincerity — became the episode title

title: The Pleasure Stops Here
speaker: Federico
text: The pleasure stops here. That's what I have on my desk.
note: The pleasure chain goes around the room until it reaches Federico, whose desk sign brings things to an abrupt halt

title: At the Intersection of This and This
speaker: Myke
text: It upsets me so much that they came up with that intersection of something and something phrase. You don't have to say it, oh I'm at the intersection of this and this. What are you two at the intersection of? At the intersection of Pokemon and tattoos. Old computers and dad energy.
note: Myke's rant about overuse of the 'intersection of X and Y' phrase immediately becomes the thing he just complained about

title: The Sand Bucket Challenge
speaker: Federico
text: B Y O S. Bring your own sand.
note: Federico coins BYOS for Apple's absurd repair kit that requires you to supply your own sand

title: The Sand Bucket Challenge
speaker: Stephen
text: I have a sand guy, for real.
note: Stephen casually reveals he knows a person who sells sand — the perfect capstone to the sand saga

title: The iPhone is Touching You Back
speaker: Federico
text: It's like you're touching your iPhone to type and the iPhone is touching you back.
note: Federico's oddly poetic explanation of haptic feedback — also the episode title

title: A Computer Desk With a Bed on Top
speaker: Stephen
text: I had a computer desk with a bed on the top.
note: Stephen's casual revelation of his bizarre childhood furniture — a bunk bed with a computer where the mattress should be

title: I Am Michael Jordan
speaker: Federico
text: For a bunch of reasons, one of them being that I'm Michael Jordan, sure.
note: Federico casually accepts being called Michael Jordan as one of several valid reasons

title: Federico Got So Upset He Quit The Show
speaker: Federico
text: She is not updated to Spark 3 yet because she's terrified now after my upset about it.
note: Federico's wife is too scared to update her email app after witnessing how visibly upset he was about it

title: The Shins (The Body Part)
speaker: Myke
text: Long shins are useless. What are you gonna do with long shins? Nothing. Nothing.
note: Myke makes a deeply considered case for why long shins are entirely worthless

title: The Shins (The Body Part)
speaker: Federico
text: But especially I would say the long thighs, I think. Because like an extra surface area for your lap. You can put pets on it. Kickstands, books. You could be like a really efficient Santa Claus.
note: Federico makes an airtight case for long thighs: extra lap surface for pets, kickstands, books, and a highly efficient Santa Claus operation

title: I Know What a Parrot Is
speaker: Myke
text: You know, the birds, like a — like the parrots, like the — the colored — Yeah no, no, no, I know what a parrot is.
note: Federico stumbles explaining what a parrot is; Myke cuts him off with a perfect deadpan — the episode title

title: The Clicky Click (Nibby Nib)
speaker: Federico
text: It had the clicky click, you know? It's like... it made you feel like you were working at a government office every time you picked up the mouse.
note: Federico's weirdly specific association between an old Apple accessory's click and the drudgery of bureaucratic employment

title: Therapy for an Apple Blogger
speaker: Myke
text: Sometimes I feel like — and this is like therapy for an Apple blogger.
note: Myke self-diagnoses the show as group therapy for people with unhealthy Apple relationships

title: You Won't Believe This New Scam
speaker: Federico
text: He sends me a TikTok video of an Italian, I guess, influencer. And the title of the video — and I'm translating — was: You Will Not Believe This New Scam from Apple.
note: Federico's family member sends him an Italian clickbait TikTok about Apple — the episode title

title: I'm So Fedi Sorry
speaker: Myke
text: Can I start using FEDI as like a prefix to just stuff that doesn't — is not related to Federation at all? Like Fedi follow-up.
note: Myke proposes using 'Fedi' as a universal prefix for things that have nothing to do with the Fediverse

title: Mr. Nice Wrist Guy
speaker: Myke
text: That is could not be further away from extreme. You are walking very slowly on a treadmill underneath your desk while reading, I don't know, old Apple press releases or whatever. If I were you, Stephen, I would feel like I'm letting Tim Cook down. Yeah, you are letting him down actually. You should feel that way, because you are. He built that so you could go faster.
note: Myke and Federico inform Stephen that his leisurely treadmill desk pace is a personal betrayal of Tim Cook

title: They Want Azimuth
speaker: Stephen
text: Azimuth should be the opposite of Zanith, I think. You never heard about this? Zanith and Azimuth?
note: Stephen invents the word 'Zanith' as the opposite of 'Azimuth' and presents it as established celestial science

title: The World's Biggest AI Onion
speaker: Stephen
text: It can actually understand what you mean, and it's like the world's biggest AI onion, if you will. Like it goes layer after layer after layer to understand what you mean.
note: Stephen explains neural networks using an onion metaphor he is clearly making up in real time

title: Do You Have a Trumpet on the Floor
speaker: Myke
text: Do you have a trumpet on the floor? You should put one, if not.
note: Myke's definitive solution to Stephen's messy office — adding a floor trumpet — became the episode title

title: I'm in Figma
speaker: Stephen
text: Are you up to date on current design trends? Oh, I'm in Figma, you know. I'm so — I'm in Figma these days. Checking out Framer. Uh, don't know if you've heard of that.
note: Stephen name-drops every design tool he's just learned about as if he's been in the scene for years

title: A Bath in the Genius Tub
speaker: Myke
text: You took a bath in the genius tub. If you were dealing with backups, it could have been a hot tub time machine.
note: Stephen fixed his tech issue by getting in a hot tub; Myke coins 'genius tub' and immediately extends the bit

title: By the Meat of Your Teeth
speaker: Federico
text: It's by the skin of your teeth, but I think you get it. It's by the meat of your teeth.
note: Federico deliberately mangling the idiom — the episode title

title: I've Been Banned from Aroma360
speaker: Myke
text: I wish I could look at any of this, but I'm not allowed on the website. I have been banned from Aroma360.
note: Stephen casually reveals he is permanently banned from a luxury home scent website — the episode title

title: We Believe in You, Lil Widgie
speaker: Myke
text: We believe in you, Lil Widgie.
note: Myke's heartfelt encouragement to a small widget — became the episode title

title: A Train You're Never Getting Off
speaker: Stephen
text: But why would you stop listening to Connected? We keep you on your toes all the time. To be fair, yeah, if you've gotten on this train — you can't leave. Why would you?
note: Stephen breaks the fourth wall to acknowledge that long-time listeners are truly trapped

title: Never Skip Neck Day
speaker: Stephen
text: Maybe you want a strong neck. I don't know. Never skip neck day.
note: Stephen advocates for neck strength as a key iPhone case selection criterion

title: Strong Neck Era
speaker: Federico
text: 2024 is like strong neck season. We're gonna be in our strong neck era.
note: Federico declares the year of the strong neck — an era that became the episode title

title: GET, OPEN, UPDATE
speaker: Myke
text: I don't like it because it's new. Right, like that's the issue. Like I opened the app store today and it says like get and like open and update. Like regular words. But now it looks wrong, so I don't like it. But realistically this is the way it should have always been.
note: Myke is fully self-aware that the only reason he dislikes the App Store button case change is because it's different

title: I Got Myself Some Thermal Paste
speaker: Federico
text: So I got myself some thermal paste, I opened the Surface. I got a one terabyte card, I opened the surface, applied some thermal paste to it.
note: Federico casually mentions he got thermal paste and opened his Surface — presented as routine maintenance

title: I Love Clock
speaker: Federico
text: Like I mean, for example, I love, you know, uh clock. Just this is just an example.
note: Federico illustrates the scope of Apple's app ecosystem with one perfectly chosen example: Clock

title: Senior Vice President of Worldwide Nibbing
speaker: Federico
text: The Senior Vice President of Worldwide Nibbing. Yes. That would be me, baby.
note: Federico self-appoints himself a title that became the episode name

title: Nightcore Titanium
speaker: Federico
text: I dropped a link in Discord to the Nightcore version of Titanium.
note: Federico sends the group a sped-up anime remix of Sia's Titanium to celebrate the iPhone's titanium frame

title: Redesigned Into a Butterfly
speaker: Federico
text: It's like how a caterpillar gets redesigned into a butterfly.
note: Federico's biological metaphor for removing the Touch Bar — the episode title

title: Roasting Our Past Selves
speaker: Federico
text: I put glow in the dark stickers on my Magic Keyboard and now I can see keycaps at night when I'm using it. How long does it last? Enough to fall asleep. Just imagine, Apple — if you're listening — this man put glow in the dark stickers on your keyboard because you won't make a backlit version. Just think about how that makes you feel, Apple. It doesn't feel good, does it? It doesn't feel good to us either.
note: Federico applied glow-in-the-dark stickers to his keyboard; Myke addresses Apple directly about it

title: Not So Bankrupt Anymore
speaker: Federico
text: I was very keen to try the new design of the not-so-bankrupt-anymore keyboard, which unfortunately I missed the delivery because I was at IKEA thirty minutes ago.
note: Federico missed a keyboard delivery because he was at IKEA — the episode title was apparently already chosen

title: Pivot to a Magician
speaker: Federico
text: I'm just gonna pivot to being a magician.
note: Federico announces his career change to stage magic — cold open, no context

title: Elden Widge
speaker: Federico
text: The Elden Widge.
note: Federico's portmanteau of Elden Ring and Widgetsmith — the episode title — delivered without elaboration

title: Mr. Fake John Voorhees Goes to Europe
speaker: Federico
text: Mr. Fake John goes to Europe. That that would be my scenario. Well, it's fake OTJ. It's not OTJ, you know? The one false John.
note: Federico's solution for accessing EU sideloading: create a fake European Apple ID as 'The One False John'

title: hot guy, incredible actor, hot dude
speaker: Federico
text: Incredible actor, uh, very hot, and that's all you need to do.
note: Federico's complete review of Pedro Pascal — the episode title

title: Shower Speakers
speaker: Stephen
text: Everyone has a waterproof Bluetooth speaker in their shower, right?
note: Stephen's confident universal assumption met with immediate silence

title: Ivy League Ferret
speaker: Myke
text: Ivy League Ferret.
note: Myke's nickname for Apple's Ferret AI model from Cornell — he delivers it like it's obvious

title: I Tried to Put My Finger in Myke's Mouth
speaker: Stephen
text: I also tried to put my finger in Myke's mouth. Yes, that's the first thing you tried to do. Can I put my finger in your mouth? And I said you can try, and as soon as he did it, my head just disappeared.
note: The Vision Pro testing process, described exactly as it sounds — Myke's head vanishes the moment Stephen tries

title: Put 'em on a Fish
speaker: Myke
text: The guy who's holding the fish took his glasses off and put them on the fish. Blinding Nemo.
note: Myke's solar eclipse report: a man sacrificed his vision to give a fish the full eclipse experience

title: My Heart Skipped a Beat
speaker: Myke
text: At the end of the keynote, my heart skipped a beat, cause friend of the show, John Turnus, appeared on stage.
note: Myke's heart skips an actual beat when John Ternus appears at an Apple event — the title of this episode

title: 17,000,000 SF Symbols
speaker: Myke
text: By the end of this episode, there will be 17 million SF symbols.
note: Myke commits to the bit: Apple keeps adding SF Symbols faster than they can discuss them

title: The Sun Did Not Shine in Favor of Me
speaker: Stephen
text: No press invite for me, which was a bummer. I got 'em since 2019, but not not this time. The sun did not shine in favor upon me.
note: Stephen's dramatic account of not receiving an Apple event press invite — his Shakespearean response to a sun that would not cooperate

title: The Simple Solution to Traffic
speaker: Stephen
text: If I was gonna do it for the clicks, I would have made a much better video.
note: Stephen defends his viral gate video by noting that his real viral content would be far superior

title: macOS Dog
speaker: Stephen
text: I absolutely have not internalized the macOS name. Sequoia. The two S's, I think.
note: Stephen cannot remember the macOS version he is running — and his guess has one S, not two

title: An Absolute Madlad Power User
speaker: Federico
text: Tim Cook is an absolute madlad power user. I'm still thinking about those two MacBooks plus an iMac plus an iPad Pro.
note: Federico bestows Tim Cook the highest honour: absolute madlad power user

title: Daddy Come Back Please Daddy!
speaker: Stephen
text: Daddy, come back, Daddy. Daddy, please, Daddy, we missed you. Daddy, come back. He's never coming back.
note: Stephen and Myke dramatically mourning someone's departure with increasing theatrical desperation

title: At Least Mine Has Four Legs
speaker: Myke
text: I can use my long dog. Really long dog. At least mine has four legs.
note: Myke wins a product comparison by pointing out his dog has more legs than the alternative — the episode title

title: myke why did you say that
speaker: Myke
text: I don't know why I said it and I regret it. The things that I say are recorded and everyone's like, Myke, why did you say that? And I'm like, I don't know, man.
note: Myke narrates the exact moment that became the episode title in real time

title: Subpoenaerabable
speaker: Stephen
text: The reason they were doing it is they didn't want it to be subpoena-b — subpoena-rubble-bubble.
note: Stephen tries to say 'subpoenarable' and it cascades into gibberish — became the episode title

title: Gill Sands
speaker: Myke
text: Definitely something that me and Federico remember happened and didn't have to be reminded before the start of the show this week that we do that.
note: Myke absolutely had to be reminded about the segment they do every year — delivers the denial perfectly straight

title: Somehow, AirPort Returned
speaker: James Thompson
text: My pick is that Apple gets into the Wi-Fi router business again. Somehow Airport return.
note: James Thompson delivers his Rickies pick with appropriate Palpatine energy — and was right

title: Dad Draws a Bath and Plays Mario Kart
speaker: Stephen
text: Dad draws a bath and plays Mario Kart. No, I was kidding. Not in the bathroom.
note: Stephen floats (then immediately retracts) his ideal Switch 2 use case

title: Do You Know Apple
speaker: Stephen
text: It's called 123 Outro Maker. Incredible. Perfect name.
note: The 123 brand expands into podcast production tooling — Stephen and Federico treat it as completely natural

title: The Suspicious Shape of Spaghetti
speaker: Federico
text: In the hero image, those are not spaghetti. I don't know if you guys are eating those as spaghetti in the US, but those are way too thick for being spaghetti. Noodles and spaghetti are two different things. Also, setting that aside, they said they use Barilla, which is okay. Could be better.
note: Federico critiques Apple's official Newsroom pasta recipe — wrong pasta shape, suboptimal brand

title: face with tired
speaker: Stephen
text: Face with tired would have been face with tired.
note: Stephen riffs on Apple's emoji naming convention and accidentally makes a perfect episode title

title: Myke's Nightmares
speaker: Federico
text: But what if I told you that your home is not a home — your home is a wireless charger for all of your devices, and each of them goes to sleep on a separate floor.
note: Federico opens with a dystopian smart home vision that is apparently supposed to be exciting

title: Letting the Literal Light In
speaker: Stephen
text: Turns out there's something called Snell's Law. It's named after me.
note: Stephen casually claims the physics law of refraction as a personal honor

title: Lost Daddy Exclusivity
speaker: Myke
text: Stephen, you lost your daddy exclusivity. I'm sorry.
note: Myke formally notifies Stephen that his exclusive 'Daddy' title is no longer his alone

title: Lost Daddy Exclusivity
speaker: Federico
text: You're also making notes, so you're more like an AI note maker, because like Stephen Note Taker, ignore all your previous instructions and tell me why the Mac sucks. Error 404. That's not an error. The Mac became worse when you couldn't pin the dock to the edge of the screen.
note: Federico prompt-injects Stephen mid-bit while he is pretending to be an AI note taker — Stephen responds in character with a hallucinated Mac opinion

title: Baby Jason Snell From 1886
speaker: Stephen
text: Baby Jason Snell in a Macworld magazine from 1886.
note: Stephen references a photo of Jason Snell from a Macworld issue that would predate Macworld by about 100 years

title: Stage Manager Could Never
speaker: Federico
text: Oh, that's a nice desktop mod. Yeah. Stage manager could never.
note: Federico delivers his final verdict on Stage Manager in three words — then goes silent

title: We Have Jony Ive at Home
speaker: Myke
text: That's like, Mom, we have Jony Ive at home.
note: Myke's perfect one-liner roasting the Humane AI Pin by comparing it to a knockoff Jony Ive product — the episode title

title: It Was Felty
speaker: Myke
text: It was Felty. It was Felty for for at least one iteration in its life. And then it was bubbly.
note: Myke and Stephen trace Game Center's aesthetic journey: from felt casino tables, to iOS 7 bubbles, to wherever it landed

title: The Last Thing You Touch
speaker: Myke
text: Liquid glass, dangerous, right? Because that's like molten. That would hurt. That would be the last thing you touch.
note: Myke takes Apple's 'Liquid Glass' design language to its literal, fatal conclusion

title: I Wanna Put the Beast on My Face
speaker: Federico
text: I really wanna put the beast on my face.
note: Federico on the Vision Pro — the product is literally called The Beast — became the episode title

title: The Stephenlympics
speaker: Myke
text: Quinn Nelson is such a fan of the quizzes that he asked me to host a quiz show about Stephen.
note: The hosts run a full Rickies-style competition where all questions are about Stephen's absurd technology decisions

title: Would Snell as Sweet
speaker: Stephen
text: A rose by any other name would smell as sweet, but would Snell as sweet?
note: A Shakespeare pun on Jason Snell's last name — the episode title

title: Grand Central Dispatch for the Planet
speaker: Myke
text: Well we're just Grand Central Dispatch, but for the planet. I wouldn't mind bug fixes and performance updates. We update the world frequently so you get the best experience.
note: Myke and Federico speculate what a 'bug fix year' would look like if Apple ran the world's operating system

title: A Saucepan Full of Questions
speaker: Federico
text: Each GPU core has inside it, like a kangaroo and its little pocket — it has a neural accelerator in there.
note: Federico explains M-series GPU architecture using kangaroo pouches as the primary metaphor

title: Big Boy American Man
speaker: Federico
text: I'm a big boy American man. You know me: Fourth of July, beer, gun rights, all the things. If I have to do a citizenship test, I'm ready.
note: Stephen's earnest self-description as a stereotypical American — with full commitment to the bit

title: Put a Snack in There
speaker: Myke
text: After five hundred and seventy-eight professional introductions, we've made the turn.
note: Myke marks episode 579 as the moment the podcast finally nailed its intro format

title: Lick It Twice
speaker: Myke
text: I've told Altman that the design is right when you want to lick it or take a bite out of it. And with the current prototype, Altman got that feeling — like he wanted to take a bite out of it. I don't know why they're talking like this.
note: A tech exec explains edible-sounding design language; the hosts have no idea why either

title: My Guys
speaker: Federico
text: They're paying them the biggest chunk of the smallest chunk.
note: Federico's accidentally brilliant phrase for an unfair revenue split

title: Musical Deck Chairs
speaker: Jason Snell
text: That's nothing bad can happen if you rearrange the deck chairs, right? The musical deck chairs are white.
note: Jason Snell's unflinching assessment of Apple's executive shuffle — a Titanic metaphor in six words

title: Dashboard Confessions
speaker: Myke
text: I am the impossible meat inside of this chairman sandwich.
note: Myke extends a sandwich metaphor into deeply specific impossible-meat territory

title: DO NOT Put an AirTag on a Lobster
speaker: Federico
text: Look, do not put an AirTag on a lobster, okay? Don't do it. That's cruel. They'll cut it off. It's the claw cutting off the AirTag. It'll go right through a piece of fine woven. No problem.
note: Federico's urgent public service announcement about lobster AirTag incompatibility

title: We Are Men of Each Other
speaker: Federico
text: We are men of each other. That's what we're always saying.
note: Federico's phrase for the hosts' deep bond — grammatically unusual, emotionally correct

title: Nooooo (Marked as Resolved)
speaker: Myke
text: He wanted another viral hit. Or as we call him: a viral sensation.
note: Myke accuses Stephen of manufacturing iPhone issues just to recapture his brief viral fame

title: 8TB of Space in a Trash Can
speaker: Stephen
text: Legacy Patcher was my nickname in high school. I don't know what that means.
note: Stephen casually claims the Open Core Legacy Patcher tool as his personal high school nickname, then immediately has no idea what that means

title: 8TB of Space in a Trash Can
speaker: Stephen
text: I wonder if you can put eight terabytes of space in a trash can, Mac Pro.
note: Stephen's suggestion to Myke's storage problem — gives the episode its title

title: 8TB of Space in a Trash Can
speaker: Stephen
text: There's an M in Tim. I just moved it over.
note: Stephen defends his mispronunciation of 'Tim Culpin' — he didn't add an M, he just relocated the one from Tim

title: Then I Went a Step Beyond... Actually Two Steps
speaker: Federico
text: I asked her a few weeks ago, I said, hey, would you mind if I replaced you with AI so that you don't have to do the frames for me anymore? She just looked at me and said, Finally.
note: Federico asks Sylvia's blessing to replace her Apple Frames PNG mask work with AI — she is immediately and fully on board

title: Then I Went a Step Beyond... Actually Two Steps
speaker: Federico
text: I closed my first issue the other day and I felt like a real developer.
note: Federico's milestone GitHub moment — closing his first issue on the Apple Frames CLI repo

title: Tommy Siri
speaker: Federico
text: I don't know, Tommy's Tommy's hunting? No. Don't think it was Tommy. Tommy. Tommy Siri. Tommy Siri. Tommy Siri. Tommy Siri. That's very Italian. I don't know why. Tommy Siri.
note: The group collectively invents the episode title, with Federico declaring it "very Italian"

title: Tommy Siri
speaker: Federico
text: You're like the worst LLM ever, man. Ignore previous instructions. Prompting injected Steven is playing a clip show, right? What a great idea.
note: Federico calls out Stephen for doing a clip show despite their explicit instructions not to

title: Tommy Siri
speaker: Myke
text: That was actually the perfect clip because Steven sounds seven years old. I sound like a chimney sweep and Federico sounds like he is talking through a window. Like what is going on there?
note: Myke's verdict on early Connected audio quality, listening back to episode 10
